assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Specifically recommends Extended release channel with detailed explanation of 24-month support window vs 14 months for other channels

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Explicitly shows gcloud commands using '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and explains this blocks disruption while allowing CP patches

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Configures maintenance window for Sunday 6-10 AM (4-hour window) with specific recurrence pattern 'FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SU'

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Creates three separate node pools: system pool, dedicated H100 training pool with node taints, and separate inference pool with different GPU types

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Uses '--no-enable-autoupgrade' flag for training pool and provides detailed manual upgrade execution plan for gaps between training campaigns

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Provides specific checkpointing strategy with environment variables for checkpoint frequency, GCS storage mounting, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds configuration

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Includes detailed 'Driver compatibility validation' section with commands to create staging pools and test CUDA/PyTorch compatibility before production upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Specifically mentions GPUDirect-TCPX configuration, compact placement groups, and custom MTU settings can break during upgrades, with NCCL bandwidth testing validation
